item.title: EUAM Ukraine 1-2026 Call for Contributions for the European Union Advisory Mission for Civilian Security Sector Reform Ukraine
item.description: Teaser Deadline for applications: Wednesday, 29 April 2026, at 17:00 (Brussels time) Text Job location/s: As indicated in Annex 1 1. You have the nationality of an EU Member State: you must use Goalkeeper to apply: a) You are already registered on Goalkeeper AND you have an EU Login: https://goalkeeper.eeas.europa.eu/registrar/web b) You do not have a Goalkeeper account or an EU Login: https://goalkeeper.eeas.europa.eu/registrar/web/DPA/357/details.do c) Candidates from contributing third states: Please use the application form (Annex 3) Only seconded nationals of a non-EU Contributing Third State can be proposed by their National Seconding Authority (no personal applications will be considered): Please contact your seconding authority to send them your application form. Please note: Seconded positions are only available for candidates already validated in the database of their Seconding Authority. Please contact your National Seconding Authority for more information on applying for vacant seconded positions. We cannot provide contact details of National Seconding Authorities. 2. For more information relating to selection and recruitment, please contact the Civilian Operations Headquarters (CivOpsHQ): Mr Bruno FERREIRA via: CivOpsHQ-HR-EUAM-Ukraine@eeas.europa.eu
item.dc:creator: European External Action Service

item.title: EEAS Vacancy: Funded Traineeship for young graduates at the EU Delegation to Türkiye-Press and Info Section
item.description: Funded traineeship for young graduates at the EU Delegation to Türkiye Are you a young graduate interested in international relations? Are you curious to learn more about work of a diplomatic mission and how the EU Delegation represents EU interests and values in Türkiye? We offer: A traineeship of 6 months in 2026 within the Press and Information Section of the EU Delegation to Türkiye. The tentative start date is 01 July 2026. The Press and Information Section of the EU Delegation is the EU’s main communication arm in the host country. Its role is to explain the European Union’s policies, values, and actions, and to promote a clear and coherent understanding of EU–host country relations among the public, media, and key stakeholders. It acts as the primary interface with journalists, providing timely information through press releases, briefings, interviews, and media monitoring, while also managing sensitive or crisis communication when necessary. The section designs and implements the Delegation’s overall communication strategy, ensuring consistent messaging aligned with EU institutions and Member States. It manages the Delegation’s digital presence, including the website and social media platforms. At the same time, it ensures the visibility of EU-funded programmes and projects by highlighting their impact and applying EU branding standards in cooperation with implementing partners. Through public diplomacy and outreach activities—such as events, debates, cultural initiatives, and high-level visits—the Press and Information Section strengthens dialogue, transparency, and trust, while also reporting back to Brussels on media trends and public opinion in the host country Main tasks : Support the Communication team with the preparation, implementation and follow-up of public events and campaigns organised by the Delegation (both internal and external), including those related to COP 31 Attend coordination meetings and draft minutes, liaise with other teams across the EU Delegation and with external partners as part of communication team’s activities Contribute to operating the Delegation’s website and social media Support the team with the administrative and other relevant tasks We look for: Qualifications or special requirements : Recent university degree, ideally in public communications A knack for communication and organisation of events Familiarity with web, social media and relevant platforms and tools Knowledge of the EU and its relations with Türkiye Knowledge of Turkish language is an asset How to apply? Candidates must apply to the e-mail address DELEGATION-TURKIYE-TRAINEE-APPLICATIONS@eeas.europa.eu and send the following documents: Please mention in the subject of your e-mail : Funded Traineeship - PRESS INFO Important information to read before applying Before applying you are requested to examine the dedicated website as well as the general eligibility criteria for a paid traineeship (Article 8 of the Decision ADMIN(2017)28 – Paid traineeship). Each selected trainee will receive a monthly grant to cover living expenses. All costs related to travels, visa, insurance, accommodation must be borne by the trainee. Applications from non-eligible candidates will not be considered. After the evaluation of all applications, shortlisted candidates will be contacted and invited for an interview. Unsuccessful candidates will be notified by email. In case the selected candidates are not able to present the required documents, their application will be rejected. The traineeships can only be offered in compliance with the local legislation as well as the legislation of the country of origin. Citizens from EU Member states: must provide proof that they will be covered against the risk of a pandemic and the costs of repatriation; must hold a relevant entrance visa and / or temporary residence permit where applicable, based on the traineeship agreement, the duration of the traineeship, and in compliance with the local legislation.
item.dc:creator: European External Action Service

item.title: Funded traineeship for young graduates at the EU Delegation to Ecuador - Political, Press and Information Section
item.description: Text We offer: A traineeshipof 6 months in 2026 within the Political, Press and Information Section of the EU Delegation to Ecuador,. The tentative start date is June 2026. Main tasks : Gather and monitor daily incoming information in the press for reports Follow-up and analyse political developments in the country Investigate, compile information on specific topics of interest for the Delegation, draft background notes Contribute to preparing content for the EU delegation website and other relevant websites, as well as content for the Delegation social media Assist in the organization of EU events (Europe Day and others) or events supported by the EU Prepare content for key messages and speeches Take part in meetings, seminars and events & report back Any other requests from the Head of Section and the Head of Delegation. We look for: Qualifications or special requirements : Considering the local health and security conditions, only applicants currently residing in Ecuador will be considered. BA degree in Political Science, International relations, communications or related Excellent communication skills in both English and Spanish Good knowledge or specialization in Latin America Good analytical and drafting skills Ability to handle politically sensitive issues in a responsible manner How to apply? Candidates must apply to the e-mail address DELEGATION-ECUADOR-RECRUITMENT@eeas.europa.eu and send the following documents: Please mention in the subject of your e-mail : Funded Traineeship - Your name / Press, Political and Information Section Important information to read before applying Before applying you are requested to examine the dedicated website as well as the general eligibility criteria for a paid traineeship (Article 8 of the Decision ADMIN(2017)28 – Paid traineeship). Each selected trainee will receive a monthly grant to cover living expenses. All costs related to travels, visa, insurance, accommodation must be borne by the trainee. Applications from non-eligible candidates will not be considered. After the evaluation of all applications, shortlisted candidates will be contacted and invited for an interview. Unsuccessful candidates will be notified by email. In case the selected candidates are not able to present the required documents, their application will be rejected. The traineeships can only be offered in compliance with the local legislation as well as the legislation of the country of origin. Citizens from EU Member states: must provide proof that they will be covered against the risk of a pandemic and the costs of repatriation; must hold a relevant entrance visa and / or temporary residence permit where applicable, based on the traineeship agreement, the duration of the traineeship, and in compliance with the local legislation.
item.dc:creator: European External Action Service

item.title: Funded traineeship for young graduates at the EU Delegation to Chile
item.description: Funded traineeship for young graduates at the EU Delegation to Chile The deadline for submitting applications is 17/04/2026 Are you a young graduate interested in international relations? Are you curious to learn more about work of a diplomatic mission and how the EU Delegation represents EU interests and values in Chile? We offer: A traineeshipof 6 months in 2026 within the Political, Press & Information Section of the EU Delegation,. The tentative start date is June 2026. Insert description of the Section (max. 10 lines - not mandatory, delete as appropriate) Main tasks : Monitor the media and public debates and contribute to the daily press reviews; Monitor developments in Chile in order to contribute to analysis and writing of reports on national and international issues related to politics, economy, trade, society and culture; Follow-up the implementation of bilateral EU-Chile agreements, and in particular the Advanced Framework Agreement and the Interim Trade Agreement Support the preparation of meetings with external interlocutors and the organization of missions and high-level visits; Contribute to the organisation of public diplomacy activities (public events, social media campaigns etc.); Contribute to the development and implementation of institutional communication strategies and campaigns on social networks and the delegation's website. We look for: Qualifications or special requirements : University degree in law, economics, political science, international relations, or other relevant fields. A master's degree is an asset. Excellent spoken and written English and Spanish; Excellent analytical and drafting skills; Teamwork abilities; Good IT skills. How to apply? Candidates must apply to the e-mail address DELEGATION-CHILE-TRAINEES-APPLICATIONS@eeas.europa.eu and send the following documents: Please mention in the subject of your e-mail : Funded Traineeship - LAST NAME- NAME + TRAINEESHIP PPI Important information to read before applying Before applying you are requested to examine the dedicated website as well as the general eligibility criteria for a paid traineeship (Article 8 of the Decision ADMIN(2017)28 – Paid traineeship). Each selected trainee will receive a monthly grant to cover living expenses. All costs related to travels, visa, insurance, accommodation must be borne by the trainee. Applications from non-eligible candidates will not be considered. After the evaluation of all applications, shortlisted candidates will be contacted and invited for an interview. Unsuccessful candidates will be notified by email. In case the selected candidates are not able to present the required documents, their application will be rejected. The traineeships can only be offered in compliance with the local legislation as well as the legislation of the country of origin. Citizens from EU Member states: must provide proof that they will be covered against the risk of a pandemic and the costs of repatriation; must hold a relevant entrance visa and / or temporary residence permit where applicable, based on the traineeship agreement, the duration of the traineeship, and in compliance with the local legislation.
item.dc:creator: European External Action Service

item.title: Funded traineeship for young graduates at the EU Delegation to Vietnam
item.description: Text We offer: Two 6-month traineeships in 2026 within the Political, Press and Information Section (PPI) Section of the EU Delegation to Vietnam. The tentative start date is June 2026. Main tasks : We look for: Qualifications or special requirements: How to apply? Candidates must submit the following documents to DELEGATION-VIETNAM-VACANCY@eeas.europa.eu A detailed standard Europass format CV (please see here for Europass template); CVs in other formats will not be considered; A cover letter describing why you wish to participate in a EU traineeship; An application form . Please state in the subject of your e-mail : Funded Traineeship - Name/PPI Important information to read before applying: Before applying you are requested to examine the dedicated website as well as the general eligibility criteria for a paid traineeship (Article 8 of the Decision ADMIN(2017)28 – Paid traineeship). Each selected trainee will receive a monthly grant to cover living expenses. All costs related to travels, visa, insurance, accommodation must be borne by the trainee. Applications from non-eligible candidates will not be considered. After the evaluation of all applications, shortlisted candidates will be contacted and invited for an interview. Unsuccessful candidates will be notified by email. In case the selected candidates are not able to present the required documents, their application will be rejected. The traineeships can only be offered in compliance with the local legislation as well as the legislation of the country of origin. Citizens from EU Member States must: provide proof that they will be covered against the risk of a pandemic and the costs of repatriation; hold a relevant entrance visa and / or temporary residence permit where applicable, based on the traineeship agreement, the duration of the traineeship, and in compliance with the local legislation.
item.dc:creator: European External Action Service

item.title: EU Twinning Project is looking for a Language Assistant
item.description: Text EU Twinning Project UA 23 NDICI FI 01 24 ‘Implementing effective supervision over non-bank financial institutions’ is an EU funded project that is jointly implemented by a consortium of institutions from Poland, Lithuania and Spain, namely Urząd Komisji Nadzoru Finansowego (Polish Financial Supervision Authority - UKNF), Lietuvos bankas (Bank of Lithuania - LB), Fundación para la Internacionalización de las Administraciones Públicas, F.S.P. (Foundation for the Internationalisation of Public Administrations, F.S.P. – FIAP) cooperating with Dirección General de Seguros y Fondos de Pensiones (Directorate General for Insurance and Pension Funds, Ministry of Economy, Commerce and Business of Spain - DGSFP)) for the National Bank of Ukraine (NBU). To assist the Resident Twinning Adviser with language-related tasks, the project is looking for a qualified specialist for the following position: Interested Candidates are invited to send their Application in English (including CV and all required documents as stipulated in the terms of reference) marked "Language assistant" to the following e-mail address: joanna.lipowczan@knf.gov.pl by deadline: 24 April 2026.
item.dc:creator: European External Action Service

item.title: Funded traineeship for young graduates at the EU Delegation to the Federal Republic of Nigeria and ECOWAS
item.description: Are you a young graduate interested in international relations? Are you curious to learn more about work of a diplomatic mission and how the EU Delegation represents EU interests and values in Nigeria? We offer: A traineeship of up to 6 months in 2026 within the Political, Press and Information Section (PPI) Section of the EU Delegation to Nigeria. The tentative start month is July 2026. Traineeship in the PPI Section: The PPI Section of the EU Delegation is in charge of supporting the Head of Delegation in maintaining and advancing political relations with Nigeria and ECOWAS, analysing and reporting on political developments, engaging with government and local authorities, civil society organizations, think tanks and the media. PPI also supports the Head of Delegation in his/her capacity as observer to ECOWAS, monitors regional developments and reports on related issues. The PPI Section assists the Head of Delegation in ensuring EU Coordination with Member States and in charge of coordination on political – including regional, consular, migration and security matters. Main tasks : Draft reports on internal political developments, human rights, economic situation, regional issues, foreign policy and/or other policy areas of relevance for the EU interests and values; Support the PPI Section in organisation of meetings/drafting the meeting minutes; Support the PPI Section on communication activities; Support the PPI Section on public diplomacy events; Support the PPI section on preparation of visits from EEAS HQ and other EU institutions. We look for: Qualifications or special requirements : Citizens of Nigeria Taking into account the local health and security conditions, only applicants currently residing in Abuja - Nigeria will be considered Young graduates with at least a Bachelor Diploma (or equivalent) Candidates must have less than one year of professional experience after their graduation by the signing of the Traineeship agreement . (other traineeships or student jobs or volunteering works carried out during university or after graduation are not considered as professional experience) No previous experience of more than six weeks in the EU Institutions How to apply? Candidates must apply to the e-mail address Delegation-Nigeria-Recruitment@eeas.europa.eu and send the following documents: Please mention in the subject of your e-mail : Funded Traineeship - PPI Important information to read before applying Before applying you are requested to examine the dedicated website as well as the general eligibility criteria for a paid traineeship (Article 8 of the Decision ADMIN(2017)28 – Paid traineeship). Each selected trainee will receive a monthly grant to cover living expenses. All costs related to travels, visa, insurance, accommodation must be borne by the trainee. Applications from non-eligible candidates will not be considered. After the evaluation of all applications, shortlisted candidates will be contacted and invited for an interview. Unsuccessful candidates will be notified by email. In case the selected candidates are not able to present the required documents, their application will be rejected. The traineeships can only be offered in compliance with the local legislation
item.dc:creator: European External Action Service

item.title: Delegation Türkiye is looking for : Programme Officer – CA FGIV
item.description: Text Place of Employment: Türkiye, Ankara Contact point: Maja Urbanska ( maja.urbanska@eeas.europa.eu ), Head of the Political, Press and Information section Deadline for application: 21/04/2026 Job title: Programme Officer Unit: ENEST.C.3.DEL.Turkiye.001.95 Function Group: FG IV Job ID: 511149 Living-conditions allowance: 15% Job description: JD CAIV 511149 - Programme Officer Applications: Applications ( CV & motivation letter) to be sent to: delegation-turkiye-contract-agents-applications@eeas.europa.eu with copy to maja.urbanska@eeas.europa.eu Please indicate your CAST Application ID in your application and mention the post number in subject line. The Contract Agent will work under the supervision of the Head of the Political section. The Contract Agent will contribute to the coordination of the Team Communication as part of the Political, Press and Information section; contribute to the development and implementation of the communication strategy of the EU Delegation; ensure maximum impact and sound financial management; act as Information and Communication Officer. Security Clearance is compulsory for all EU Staff in delegations and has to be requested by the selected candidate before departure. General Eligibility Requirements All applicants need to meet at least three general eligibility criteria: enjoy full rights as a citizen of a Member State of the EU , have fulfilled any obligations imposed by national laws concerning military service, meet the character requirements for the duties concerned. EU institutions, agencies and bodies may add additional eligibility criteria
item.dc:creator: European External Action Service

item.title: ICMPD TASLE: National ICT Expert, Sarajevo
item.description: Call for Experts National Short-Term ICT Expert TASLE Posting Date: 10/04/2026 Reference number: CfE 0426/F521/National ICT Expert Deadline for Applications: 22/04/2026 Duty Station: Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina Contract Duration: Three months THIS CALL IS OPEN TO INDEPENDENT CONTRACTORS ONLY ______________________________________________________________________________ Organisational overview The International Centre for Migration Policy Development (ICMPD) is an international organisation, with 21 Member States and working in more than 90 countries worldwide. Contributing to the development of better and safer migration policies and programmes, ICMPD takes a holistic approach to migration: structurally linking policy and research, cooperation, community outreach, and capacity development. With over 500 staff members, it takes a regional approach in its work towards efficient cooperation and partnerships along key migration routes, with a focus on Africa, Central and South Asia, Europe, Mediterranean, and the Middle East. Project background The International Centre for Migration Policy Development (ICMPD) is implementing the "Strengthening Law Enforcement Capacities to Fight Organised Crime in Bosnia and Herzegovina" project. The Overall Objective of the Action is to contribute to the establishment of effective, efficient and EU-aligned border and protection-sensitive migration management systems in BiH. Objective Component 3 of the EC-funded project provides targeted support to the Ministry of Security of Bosnia and Herzegovina (MoS), specifically its Sector for Information Technology and Telecommunication Systems (ICT). The overall purpose of Component 3 is to improve, consolidate and modernise ICT systems managed or coordinated by the MoS, thereby strengthening institutional resilience, operational efficiency and compliance with EU standards. Tasks The tasks to be completed by the National ICT Expert within this assignment are the following: To support the MoS ICT Sector by providing specialised technical expertise and expert interventions aimed at analysing, upgrading and enhancing ICT systems, ensuring alignment with EU standards and enabling improved operational capacity of law enforcement agencies, the Expert shall carry out the following tasks under Comprehensive Gap and Needs Analysis of MoS ICT Capacities with associated Development of an Action Plan for improvement subject systems in the MoS ICT Sector: - Conduct a systematic review of all relevant documentation, existing ICT systems, operational procedures, workflows and institutional structures within MoS, including any subsidiary bodies, in accordance with Activity 7.2 of the Description of Action. - Identify and clearly articulate existing functional, technical and procedural gaps affecting the effectiveness, efficiency and compliance of MoS ICT capacities. - Prepare and submit a comprehensive, evidence-based Gap and Needs Analysis Report, ensuring the report is validated through consultations with ICMPD and MoS representatives. - Prepare an evidence-based, structured Action/Work Plan with prioritisation of action for the MoS ICT Sector that is fully aligned with the deficiencies, risks, regulatory obligations, operational relevance and priorities identified in the Gap and Needs Analysis - Present the finalised findings, conclusions and recommendations to ICMPD and the MoS in an agreed format and through an agreed presentation modality. The working modalities of the expert will be subject to agreement with ICMPD and the Ministry of Security. Deliverables The National ICT Expert is expected to provide the following deliverables: 1. Documentation Review Summary: A structured summary report outlining the review of all relevant MoS ICT documentation, existing systems, operational procedures, workflows and institutional structures, including subsidiary bodies, conducted in line with Activity 7.2 of the Description of Action. 2. Gap and Needs Analysis Report (Draft): A comprehensive draft report identifying functional, technical and procedural gaps affecting MoS ICT capacities, with preliminary recommendations for addressing identified deficiencies. 3. Gap and Needs Analysis Report (Final): A final evidence-based report, incorporating feedback from consultations with ICMPD and MoS representatives, clearly articulating gaps, needs and actionable recommendations for enhancing ICT systems, ensuring compliance with EU standards and improving operational capacity. 4. Action/Work Plan: A comprehensive Draft Action/Work Plan for the improvement of the subjected information systems under the jurisdiction of the MoS ICT Sector, grounded in the findings of the Gap and Needs Analysis and including: - A clear mapping of deficiencies, risks and priorities; - A documented framework establishing the prioritisation of actions required to strengthen MoS ICT capacities; - Demonstrates justification for each action, considering feasibility, regulatory compliance
item.dc:creator: European External Action Service

item.title: La Délégation de l’Union Européenne à Madagascar recherche: Secrétaire Logistique
item.description: Nous sommes L'Union européenne (UE) est le fruit d'un partenariat économique et politique entre 27 pays européens. Elle joue un rôle majeur sur la scène internationale, que ce soit par la diplomatie, les échanges commerciaux, l’aide au développement ou la coopération avec les organisations internationales. À l’étranger, elle est représentée par plus de 140 représentations diplomatiques, connues aussi sous le nom de délégations de l’UE, qui ont une fonction similaire à celle d’une ambassade. La Délégation de l’UE auprès de la République de Madagascar et de l’Union des Comores travaille en étroite coordination avec les ambassades et les consulats des 27 États membres de l'UE. Nous sommes une mission diplomatique à part entière et représentons l’Union européenne auprès du gouvernement malgache dans les domaines qui relèvent de la compétence de l’UE. Nous Offrons Un poste de Secrétaire Logistique (Agent local Groupe 3) dans la section administrative. L’équipe est composée de 16 personnes dont 6 chauffeurs. La durée de travail hebdomadaire est de 37,5 heures par semaine et il y a occasionnellement des heures supplémentaires. Dans le cadre de cet emploi, la personne recrutée se verra attribuer des fonctions qui dépendront des besoins de la Délégation et de l’évolution de ces besoins. Le candidat retenu travaillera sous la supervision et la responsabilité du chef de la section administrative. Pour les principales tâches et fonctions actuellement requises , veuillez consulter le sur le site web de la délégation (lien ci-dessous). Le salaire brut de base dépendra de l'expérience professionnelle pertinente et vérifiée et commence à 2.025.326 MGA. Le poste est un contrat à durée déterminée de deux ans renouvelable. Il existe un ensemble d'avantages compétitifs, notamment un 13e mois, 26 jours de congé annuel par an et 14 jours fériés, une assurance maladie et un plan d'épargne retraite. La date d'entrée en fonction est estimée au 01 juin 2026. Critères d’éligibilité et de sélection Exigences minimales obligatoires Critères d’éligibilité Atouts Critères de sélection, base d'attribution des points de mérite pour la sélection Qualifications Diplôme de niveau Bac +2 minimum en secrétariat, gestion administrative, logistique ou domaine équivalent. Diplôme ou formation en comptabilité est un atout. Experience professionnelle Minimum 3 ans d’expérience professionnelle pertinente, acquise après l’obtention du diplôme requis. Expérience professionnelle dans une mission diplomatique ou une organisation internationale est un atout. Compétences linguistiques Excellente connaissance du français (niveau C2) et du malgache. Maîtrise correcte de l’anglais (niveau au moins B1) est un atout Connaissances des outils informatiques Maîtrise des outils bureautique Microsoft et de l'informatique permettant une adaptation rapide aux nouveaux logiciels, y compris les applications spécifiques à l'Union européenne Comment soumettre votre candidature Les candidats intéressés doivent consulter le profil de poste sur le site web de la Délégation à l’adresse : https://www.eeas.europa.eu/eeas/vacancies_en?s=106&f%5B0%5D=vacancy_site%3AMadagascar Veuillez envoyer votre candidature et les documents justificatifs par courrier électronique (taille maximale 1MB - pas de fichier zip) à eeasjobs-043@eeas.europa.eu avec pour objet du message : « [JP/156910] - [Nom de famille et prénom]. Le dossier doit comprendre une lettre de motivation, un CV détaillé, en format europass ( https://europa.eu/europass/en/create-europass-cv ), et une référence. En fonction du nombre de candidatures reçues, les différentes étapes de la sélection pourront inclure une présélection des candidats sur la base des informations fournies dans la lettre de motivation et le CV; des épreuves écrites et des entretiens. 6 candidats parmi les meilleurs seront convoqués à la phase finale de l’entretien, ou toute autre étape finale jugée la plus appropriée. Le comité de sélection se réserve le droit d'ajuster ce nombre en fonction de la qualité des candidatures et des notes obtenues. Seuls les candidats admis à chaque étape successive seront contactés individuellement. Le recrutement du candidat retenu sera subordonné à son aptitude médicale. La Délégation ne fournira pas d'informations supplémentaires et ne discutera pas de la procédure de sélection par téléphone : veuillez adresser toute question concernant cette procédure à : DELEGATION-MADAGASCAR-RECRUTEMENT@eeas.europa.eu La date limite de dépôt des candidatures est fixée au 27 avril à 12h00. ÉGALITÉ DES CHANCES : L'Union européenne s'engage en faveur d'une politique d'égalité des chances pour tous ses employés et candidats à un emploi. En tant qu'employeur, l'UE s'engage à promouvoir l'égalité des genres, à prévenir toute forme de discrimination et à garantir une approche de tolérance zéro à l'égard de toute forme de harcèlement. Elle accueille activement les candidatures de tous les candidats qualifiés issus de divers horizons. Nous v
item.dc:creator: European External Action Service
